name: Pizza at Last
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the topping: Core and seed the red peppers, then cut into strips. Place the peppers on an aluminum foil-lined baking sheet, skin side up, and broil until the skin is blackened (8 to 10 minutes). Remove from heat and let cool. Peel off the skins.
2. Cut tomatoes into sections without removing seeds and juice. Puree 1/3 of the peppers, tomatoes, and garlic in a blender or food processor. Repeat with the remaining ingredients two or more times.
3. Transfer the tomato-pepper mixture to a large pan. Add honey and salt. Cook over high heat until the mixture boils, then reduce heat and simmer uncovered until the sauce reaches a thick consistency (at least 1 hour).
4. Stir in red pepper flakes and fresh basil leaves.
5. Prepare the dough: In a large bowl, dissolve yeast in half a cup of warm water until the dough achieves a consistency similar to cake frosting (it should spread but not run).
6. Grease a jelly roll or baking pan that is 10 by 15 inches. Pour the batter into the center and spread evenly with a spatula into a circle. Raise the edges slightly to hold the topping sauce and sprinkle with Parmesan cheese.
7. Bake at 425°F until done (25 to 30 minutes). Makes 4 to 6 servings.
